correction_notes: Output was good quality — skill files are correct. Minor: reference files were thin but usable. No code errors.
task_prompt:
# Skill Factory Sub-system 2: Data Scraper / Ingestor Skill

**Priority**: normal
**Assignee**: claude-terminal
**Initiator**: Ashish
**Worker**: ollama
**TaskType**: code

---

## Objective

Build a Claude Code skill called `data-scraper` for the skills library at `~/ashish-skills/`. This is Sub-system 2 of the Skill Factory Pipeline (Sub-system 1 = skill-builder, already complete).

The skill lets Ashish say "scrape this URL" or "ingest this file" and get back structured, usable data — ready to feed into ARES agents, SEO reports, or other skills.

---

## Skill Location

Create the full skill directory at:
```
~/ashish-skills/data-scraper/
  SKILL.md        ← main skill prompt file
  schema.md       ← data output schemas
  references/
    scraper_patterns.md    ← common scraping patterns
    ingest_formats.md      ← supported input types
```

The skill is invoked via Claude Code: `/data-scraper`

---

## SKILL.md — What the Skill Does

The skill handles 4 input types:

### 1. URL Scrape
Input: `--url https://example.com`
- Fetch the page (plain HTTP GET)
- Strip HTML tags, scripts, styles
- Extract: title, meta description, h1-h3 headings, main body text, all links
- Output structured Markdown

### 2. Sitemap Ingest
Input: `--sitemap https://example.com/sitemap.xml`
- Fetch and parse the XML sitemap
- Extract all `<loc>` URLs with `<lastmod>` dates
- Output: flat list of URLs sorted by lastmod (newest first)
- Max 200 URLs

### 3. File Ingest
Input: `--file /path/to/file.txt` (or .md, .json, .csv)
- Read the file
- For CSV: parse into array of objects, summarize columns + row count
- For JSON: pretty-print and summarize structure
- For TXT/MD: extract headings and word count
- Output structured summary

### 4. Bulk URL List
Input: `--list /path/to/urls.txt` (one URL per line)
- Scrape each URL sequentially (max 20)
- Output one Markdown block per URL

---

## Output Format (all modes)

```markdown
## Data Scraper Output
**Source:** <url or file>
**Type:** <url|sitemap|file|bulk>
**Date:** <YYYY-MM-DD>

### Extracted Data
<structured content>

### Summary
<2-3 sentence summary of what was found>
```

---

## SKILL.md Structure

Follow the exact same pattern as `~/ashish-skills/skill-builder/SKILL.md`:
- Frontmatter block (name, version, description, triggers, output)
- Step-by-step instructions Claude follows
- Progressive disclosure (reference files loaded on demand)
- Token-efficient (main file < 300 lines)

---

## Constraints

- Skill is for Claude Code — pure prompt instructions, no Node.js runtime
- Claude uses WebFetch tool (already available in Claude Code) for URL fetching
- Claude uses Read tool for file ingestion
- No external dependencies
- Output always saved to a file when `--output /path/to/file.md` is specified
- JavaScript/web-safe — works in any Claude Code session

---

## Output

Write all file contents to:
`/Users/ashishuzelman/rank-higher-media/ares/agent_outbox/task_skill_data_scraper_complete.md`

Format:
```
# Task Complete: Data Scraper Skill

## ~/ashish-skills/data-scraper/SKILL.md
[full file content]

## ~/ashish-skills/data-scraper/schema.md
[full file content]

## ~/ashish-skills/data-scraper/references/scraper_patterns.md
[full file content]

## ~/ashish-skills/data-scraper/references/ingest_formats.md
[full file content]

## Summary
[2-3 sentences]
```

# Task Complete: Data Scraper Skill ## ~/ashish-skills/data-scraper/SKILL.md ```markdown --- name: data-scraper version: 1.0 description: Scrape URLs, ingest files, and process sitemaps to extract structured data. triggers: - scrape-url: "scrape this URL" - ingest-file: "ingest this file" - parse-sitemap: "parse this sitemap" - bulk-url-list: "scrape these URLs" output: format: markdown save-to: /path/to/file.md --- ## Step-by-Step Instructions 1. **Trigger Identification** - Identify the trigger type from the user input. 2. **URL Scrape** - If `scrape-url` is triggered, fetch the URL using WebFetch. - Strip HTML tags, scripts, and styles. - Extract title, meta description, h1-h3 headings, main body text, and all links. - Output structured Markdown. 3. **Sitemap Ingest** - If `parse-sitemap` is triggered, fetch the sitemap using WebFetch. - Parse the XML content to extract `` URLs with `` dates. - Sort URLs by last modification date (newest first). - Output a flat list of up to 200 URLs. 4. **File Ingest** - If `ingest-file` is triggered, read the file using Read tool. - For CSV files: parse into an array of objects and summarize columns + row count. - For JSON files: pretty-print and summarize structure. - For TXT/MD files: extract headings and word count. - Output structured summary. 5. **Bulk URL List** - If `bulk-url-list` is triggered, read the file using Read tool to get a list of URLs. - Scrape each URL sequentially (max 20). - Output one Markdown block per URL. 6. **Output Formatting** - Format all outputs in Markdown with structured data and summary sections. - Save output to a specified file if `--output` is provided. ## Progressive Disclosure - Load reference files on demand: - `references/scraper_patterns.md` for common scraping patterns. - `references/ingest_formats.md` for supported input types. ## Token-Efficient Design - Keep the main SKILL.md file under 300 lines. - Use concise and clear instructions to minimize token usage. ``` ## ~/ashish-skills/data-scraper/schema.md ```markdown # Data Scraper Output Schemas ## URL Scrape Schema ```json { "source": "", "type": "url", "date": "", "data": { "title": "", "meta_description": "", "headings": { "h1": [""], "h2": [""], "h3": [""] }, "body_text": "", "links": [""] } } ``` ## Sitemap Ingest Schema ```json { "source": "", "type": "sitemap", "date": "", "data": { "urls": [ { "loc": "", "lastmod": "" } ] } } ``` ## File Ingest Schema ### CSV ```json { "source": "", "type": "file", "date": "", "data": { "columns": [""], "rows": [ {"column1": "", "column2": ""} ], "row_count": } } ``` ### JSON ```json { "source": "", "type": "file", "date": "", "data": { "structure": "" } } ``` ### TXT/MD ```json { "source": "", "type": "file", "date": "", "data": { "headings": [""], "word_count": } } ``` ## Bulk URL List Schema ```json { "source": "", "type": "bulk", "date": "", "data": [ { "url": "", "title": "", "meta_description": "", "headings": { "h1": [""], "h2": [""], "h3": [""] }, "body_text": "", "links": [""] } ] } ``` ``` ## ~/ashish-skills/data-scraper/references/scraper_patterns.md ```markdown # Common Scraping Patterns ## Extract Title ```regex (.*?) ``` ## Extract Meta Description ```regex ``` ## Extract Headings (h1-h3) ```regex

(.*?)

(.*?)

(.*?)

``` ## Extract Body Text ```regex (.*?) ``` ## Extract Links ```regex (.*?) ``` ``` ## ~/ashish-skills/data-scraper/references/ingest_formats.md ```markdown # Supported Input Types ## CSV - Delimiter: Comma (`,`), Semicolon (`;`), Tab (`\t`) - Encoding: UTF-8, ISO-8859-1 ## JSON - Format: Standard JSON with key-value pairs ## TXT/MD - Text files with optional markdown formatting ``` ## Summary The `data-scraper` skill is now fully set up with detailed schemas, reference patterns, and supported input types. It can handle URL scraping, sitemap ingestion, file processing, and bulk URL list scraping, providing structured Markdown outputs for further use in ARES agents or other skills.
